title: Christies Beach New Home Build
content:
At Madderns Place Garden & Landscaping, we’ve recently started work on a complete backyard landscaping project in Adelaide, transforming a rough, uneven yard into a structured outdoor space ready for lawn and garden installation.

This project involves several key stages including site excavation, retaining wall construction, irrigation installation, and turf preparation to ensure the finished landscape is both durable and low maintenance.

Excavation and Site Preparation

The first step of this backyard renovation was a full dig-out of the existing yard. The site contained uneven ground, weeds, and poor-quality soil, so we used excavation equipment to remove the unwanted material and reshape the area.

This stage is essential in landscaping projects as it creates a stable base for retaining walls, lawn areas, and irrigation systems. Proper site preparation ensures the finished landscape remains level and drains correctly.

VersaSmooth Retaining Wall Construction

To manage the yard’s levels and create defined landscape areas, we installed a VersaSmooth block retaining wall.

VersaSmooth blocks provide a strong structural retaining solution while also offering a modern, clean finish that suits contemporary homes. These retaining walls help stabilise the soil and create clear zones for future lawn and garden beds.

Retaining walls are an important part of many Adelaide landscaping projects, particularly on sloped or uneven properties.

Premium Loam Installation for Turf Preparation

Once the structural work was completed, we began preparing the ground with high-quality premium loam.

Premium loam provides the ideal soil base for turf installation, improving drainage, nutrient availability, and root development. This step ensures the new lawn will establish quickly and remain healthy throughout Adelaide’s hot summers.

Metal Garden Edging for Clean Landscape Lines

To define the lawn and garden areas, we installed metal landscape edging. This edging creates crisp borders between turf and garden beds, helping maintain a tidy appearance while preventing soil and mulch from spreading into the lawn.

Metal edging is a popular choice in modern landscape design because it is durable, discreet, and long-lasting.

PVC Irrigation System Installation

Before laying the turf, we are installing a PVC irrigation system throughout the backyard. A properly designed irrigation system ensures even water distribution across the lawn, helping the turf establish evenly and reducing water waste.

Automated irrigation systems are especially important in South Australia where efficient water use and drought tolerance are key considerations for healthy lawns.

Next Stage of the Project

With the groundwork well underway, the next steps will include:

• Completing the premium loam installation
• Finalising the irrigation system setup
• Preparing the surface for new turf installation
• Completing garden bed finishes and final landscaping details

Once finished, this backyard will be transformed into a clean, structured, and low-maintenance outdoor space perfect for family use and entertaining.

Landscaping Services in Adelaide

At Madderns Place Garden & Landscaping, we specialise in:

• Lawn installation and turf replacement
• Retaining walls (VersaSmooth and other systems)
• Irrigation system installation and repairs
• Garden bed construction and edging
• Full backyard and front yard landscaping transformations

If you’re planning a backyard renovation or landscaping project in Adelaide, feel free to get in touch to discuss your ideas.

title: Why Soil Preparation Is the Most Important Part of Landscaping in Adelaide
excerpt: Proper soil preparation is the foundation of any successful lawn or landscaping project. In many South Adelaide properties, soil can be compacted or poor quality, which makes it difficult for turf to establish if the ground isn’t prepared properly. By excavating the area, installing premium loam, levelling the surface, and preparing irrigation before the turf is laid, the lawn has the best chance to grow evenly and develop strong roots. Taking the time to prepare the soil correctly helps create a healthier, longer-lasting lawn that can handle South Australia’s hot, dry conditions.
content:
When people think about landscaping, they usually picture the finished result — a fresh green lawn, tidy garden beds, and clean edges. What many homeowners don’t realise is that the most important part of a successful lawn actually happens before the turf is laid. It all comes down to proper soil preparation.

Across South Adelaide areas such as Seaford, Moana, Port Noarlunga, Morphett Vale and surrounding suburbs, we often see lawns that struggle because the soil underneath hasn’t been prepared properly. At Madderns Place Garden & Landscaping, we focus on getting the groundwork right so new lawns establish properly and stay healthy for years.

Why Soil Preparation Matters

A healthy lawn needs a strong base for the roots to grow into. Many properties in South Adelaide, especially newer developments, have soil that has been compacted during construction or filled with poor-quality material.

If turf is installed directly onto this type of ground, homeowners often experience problems such as:

  • Uneven lawn surfaces

  • Poor drainage after rain

  • Dry patches during summer

  • Weak turf growth

  • Increased weeds

Preparing the soil properly helps avoid these issues and gives the lawn the best possible start.

Removing Poor Soil and Debris

The first step in many lawn installations is excavating the existing ground. This removes weeds, rubble, compacted soil, and any material that may prevent healthy root growth.

By digging out the old surface and reshaping the area, we can create the correct levels for drainage and lawn installation.

This step is particularly important in coastal areas of South Adelaide where soil conditions can vary.

Installing Premium Loam for Turf

After excavation and levelling, we install premium loam soil. This soil mix provides the nutrients and structure needed for new turf to establish quickly.

Premium loam improves:

  • Soil drainage

  • Root development

  • Moisture retention

  • Overall lawn health

For homeowners installing TifTuf or Kikuyu turf in South Adelaide, good soil preparation makes a big difference in how quickly the lawn establishes and how well it handles the summer heat.

Levelling and Shaping the Yard

Another important part of landscaping preparation is getting the levels right. The ground needs to be shaped so water drains away from the home while still creating a smooth, even lawn surface.

Proper levelling also ensures the finished lawn looks clean and professional once the turf is laid.

Installing Irrigation Systems Before Turf

Before installing the lawn, it’s also the perfect time to install irrigation systems. Many homeowners in South Adelaide choose irrigation systems to keep their lawn healthy while using water efficiently.

Installing irrigation beneath the soil allows for even water coverage across the entire lawn area, helping the turf establish faster and stay green during Adelaide’s hot summers.

Setting Your Lawn Up for Long-Term Success

Skipping soil preparation is one of the most common reasons lawns fail. Taking the time to prepare the ground properly ensures the lawn drains well, grows evenly, and requires less maintenance over time.

At Madderns Place Garden & Landscaping, we believe the key to a great lawn is doing the groundwork properly before installing turf, irrigation, and garden edging.

Landscaping and Lawn Installation in South Adelaide

We provide landscaping services across South Adelaide and surrounding suburbs, including:

  • Morphett Vale

  • Seaford

  • Moana

  • Port Noarlunga

  • Christies Beach

  • Reynella

Our services include:

  • Turf installation (TifTuf and Kikuyu)

  • Soil preparation and premium loam installation

  • Irrigation system installation

  • Retaining walls

  • Garden edging and landscaping

If you're planning a new lawn or landscaping project in South Adelaide, proper soil preparation is the best place to start.

title: Best Turf Varieties for Adelaide's Hot, Dry Climate
excerpt: Choosing the right turf variety is one of the most important decisions you'll make for your lawn. Adelaide's climate — with hot, dry summers and cold winters — demands a grass that can handle water restrictions, heat stress, and heavy foot traffic. Here's what works best in South Adelaide.
content:
Choosing the right turf variety is one of the most important decisions you'll make when installing a new lawn. Adelaide's climate is tough on grass — long, hot summers with water restrictions, cold winters, and everything in between. Plant the wrong turf and you'll spend years fighting a losing battle.

At Maddern's Place, we install turf throughout South Adelaide and we see firsthand which varieties perform well and which ones struggle. Here's our honest breakdown of the best turf options for Adelaide gardens.

TifTuf Bermuda — The Adelaide Favourite

TifTuf has quickly become one of the most popular turf choices across South Australia, and for good reason. It uses up to 38% less water than other warm-season grasses, which makes it perfectly suited to Adelaide's water restrictions and long dry summers.

TifTuf produces a fine-bladed, dense lawn that handles high foot traffic well — great for families with kids and pets. It also has excellent drought dormancy, meaning it can survive dry spells and bounce back quickly once watered.

Best for: Families, active outdoor spaces, water-conscious homeowners.

Sir Walter Buffalo — Shade Tolerant and Low Maintenance

Sir Walter Buffalo is Australia's best-selling lawn variety for a reason. It's soft underfoot, handles shade better than most warm-season grasses, and recovers well from wear and tear.

For South Adelaide homes with trees, pergolas, or buildings casting shade over part of the garden, Sir Walter is often the best choice. It requires slightly more water than TifTuf but is very forgiving if you miss an irrigation cycle.

Best for: Shaded yards, low-maintenance gardens, families wanting a soft lawn.

Kikuyu — Tough, Fast-Growing, Affordable

Kikuyu is one of the most hard-wearing, fast-establishing turf varieties available. It's the most affordable option and establishes quickly after installation, which makes it popular for large lawns and budget-conscious builds.

The trade-off is that Kikuyu grows vigorously — it will spread into garden beds and paving if not properly contained. Good metal garden edging is essential when using Kikuyu.

Best for: Large lawn areas, new builds on a budget, high-traffic areas.

Couch Grass — Ideal for Full Sun

Couch is a classic Australian lawn grass that thrives in full sun and handles wear extremely well. It creates a fine, low-growing lawn when mowed regularly and is very drought tolerant once established.

Couch does not cope well in shade — if your garden gets less than 6 hours of direct sun, a different variety is a better choice. It also goes brown during winter in colder areas of Adelaide's south.

Best for: Full-sun lawns, minimal-shade properties, sporty outdoor areas.

Soil Preparation Matters More Than Turf Variety

The most common reason new lawns fail isn't the turf variety — it's inadequate soil preparation. Before we lay any turf across South Adelaide, we excavate the site, install premium loam, grade for drainage, and set up the irrigation system. This gives the new lawn every possible advantage.

Even the best turf variety will struggle on compacted clay soil or without a proper irrigation system. If you're planning a new lawn in Morphett Vale, Hackham, Noarlunga, or anywhere across Adelaide's south, soil preparation should be your first conversation.

Get Advice for Your Specific Property

The best turf for your lawn depends on your specific conditions — sun exposure, soil type, how much foot traffic it will get, and your watering setup. We're happy to walk through the options with you during a free site visit.

Contact Maddern's Place for turf installation, soil preparation, and irrigation services across Adelaide's southern suburbs.

title: How Much Does Landscaping Cost in Adelaide? A Practical Guide
excerpt: Landscaping costs vary widely depending on the scope of work, materials, and your block's conditions. This guide breaks down typical pricing for the most common landscaping services in South Adelaide — so you know what to expect before you get a quote.
content:
One of the most common questions we get asked is: "How much will it cost?" It's a fair question, and the honest answer is that landscaping costs vary quite a bit depending on the scope of work, the size of your block, the materials you choose, and the conditions on site.

This guide gives you a realistic idea of landscaping pricing in South Adelaide so you can plan your budget and have an informed conversation when you get quotes.

Retaining Walls — $300 to $500+ per lineal metre

Retaining wall costs depend on the height, material, and the amount of earthworks required. Block retaining walls (like VersaSmooth or Besser blocks) typically cost between $300–$500 per lineal metre for a standard 600–900mm height. Taller walls requiring engineering certification will cost more.

Timber sleeper walls are generally at the lower end of the price range but have a shorter lifespan than block. Natural stone is at the premium end and suits certain garden styles well.

Most South Adelaide properties with any slope will need at least one retaining wall. Factor this in early — a well-built retaining wall is one of the best investments you can make in your outdoor space.

Turf Installation — $15 to $25+ per square metre

Turf installation pricing includes soil preparation, turf supply, and laying. For a straightforward backyard in South Adelaide, expect to pay in the range of $15–$25 per square metre all-in, depending on the turf variety and how much ground preparation is needed.

TifTuf and Sir Walter Buffalo sit at the mid-to-upper end of turf pricing. Kikuyu is the most budget-friendly option. Premium loam installation, which we recommend for all lawns, adds to the cost but significantly improves the final result.

Irrigation Systems — $1,500 to $5,000+ for a full install

A basic pop-up lawn irrigation system for an average backyard in Morphett Vale or Hackham typically costs between $1,500 and $3,000 installed. Larger or more complex systems — multiple zones, smart controllers, drip irrigation for garden beds — will sit above that range.

Installing irrigation at the same time as turf is significantly cheaper than retrofitting it later, since the ground is already prepared. We strongly recommend combining both jobs where possible.

Full Backyard Landscaping — $10,000 to $40,000+

A complete backyard transformation — retaining walls, irrigation, turf, planting, paving, and drainage — will vary widely depending on the size of the space and complexity of the job. For an average South Adelaide backyard, a comprehensive project is typically in the $15,000–$30,000 range.

New home builds on empty blocks are at the lower end (no demolition required). Established properties needing significant earthworks and removal of old structures are at the higher end.

What Affects the Final Cost?

  • Block size and shape — larger and more irregularly shaped blocks take longer and cost more.
  • Slope and level changes — sloped blocks require more earthworks and retaining structure.
  • Existing conditions — removing old concrete, trees, or structures adds to the cost.
  • Materials chosen — premium turf, natural stone, and hardwood sleepers cost more than standard alternatives.
  • Irrigation complexity — the number of zones and controller type affects pricing.

Getting a Reliable Quote in Adelaide's South

The most reliable way to understand the cost for your specific project is a free site visit. At Maddern's Place, we visit your property, assess the conditions, understand what you want, and provide a clear itemised quote — no surprises.

We service Morphett Vale, Hackham, Noarlunga, Christies Beach, and all of Adelaide's southern suburbs. Get in touch to book your free consultation.

title: Irrigation Systems Adelaide: What Every Homeowner Needs to Know
excerpt: A good irrigation system is one of the best investments you can make in your Adelaide garden. With hot, dry summers and water restrictions in South Australia, automated irrigation keeps your lawn healthy without the guesswork — or the water bill blowouts. Here's everything you need to know before you buy.
content:
If you've ever come back from a two-week holiday to find your lawn brown and crispy, or spent an entire Adelaide summer dragging hoses around the yard, you already know why irrigation systems are worth it. A well-designed irrigation system saves time, saves water, and keeps your garden looking its best through South Australia's long dry summers.

Here's what you need to know before investing in a new system for your South Adelaide property.

Why Irrigation Matters in South Australia

Adelaide is one of the driest capital cities in Australia. Summer temperatures regularly exceed 40°C, and water restrictions are a regular feature of life across South Australia. Without irrigation, most lawns in Morphett Vale, Hackham, and the wider southern suburbs will turn brown between December and March.

The right irrigation system doesn't just prevent this — it waters your garden more efficiently than hand watering. Properly designed systems deliver the right amount of water to the right areas, reducing waste and lowering your water bill compared to inefficient manual watering.

Types of Irrigation Systems

Pop-up lawn sprinklers are the most common system for residential lawns. Rotating or fixed heads pop up to water, then retract flush with the turf surface. They provide even coverage across lawn areas and are ideal for TifTuf, Kikuyu, and Buffalo grass.

Drip irrigation delivers water directly to plant root zones through a network of tubes and emitters. It's the most water-efficient option and is ideal for garden beds, veggie patches, and pots. Minimal evaporation and no wet foliage means healthier plants and less disease.

Micro-spray systems combine elements of both — small sprinklers on short stakes that provide wider coverage than drip emitters but more targeted delivery than full-sized pop-ups. Good for densely planted garden beds.

Smart Controllers — Worth the Extra Cost?

Modern smart irrigation controllers adjust watering schedules automatically based on local weather data. If it's rained, the system skips the next cycle. During a heatwave, it may increase frequency. Connected to your Wi-Fi, you can control everything from your phone.

For most South Adelaide homeowners, a smart controller pays for itself within a couple of seasons through water savings. Under SA water restrictions, using an automated system also ensures you're watering on compliant days and times.

Install Before the Turf Goes Down

The single best time to install an irrigation system is before the turf is laid. Doing it this way means we can run pipes through undisturbed ground, position heads at the correct spacing, and bury everything neatly before the lawn goes on top.

Retrofitting irrigation under existing turf is possible but more expensive and disruptive. If you're planning a new lawn or a garden renovation, always include irrigation in the initial scope of work.

How Long Does an Irrigation System Last?

A quality PVC irrigation system installed correctly will last 15–25 years or more. The heads and solenoid valves may need servicing or replacement over time, but the pipe network itself is very durable.

We recommend an annual service check — testing all zones, cleaning filters, and adjusting run times seasonally. This ensures your system runs efficiently and catches small issues before they become expensive repairs.

Irrigation Installation Across Adelaide's South

Maddern's Place designs and installs irrigation systems throughout South Adelaide — including Morphett Vale, Hackham, Christies Beach, Noarlunga, Seaford, and surrounding suburbs. We also handle repairs, upgrades, and fault-finding on existing systems.

If you're planning a new lawn, a garden renovation, or just want to upgrade your current system, get in touch for a free site assessment and quote.
